lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: LOCKED 2 Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: LOCKED 2 codes are the fastest no-cost Yen boost. Clear the tutorial first, then redeem active codes from Menu > Store > Codes before rotations replace them.

steps.title: TY5KLIKES - 15k Yen (Active)
steps.description: One of the current high-value LOCKED 2 codes and a strong early Yen injection for a fresh account.

steps.title: BUGFIX01 - 10k Yen (Active)
steps.description: Live code tied to the latest LOCKED 2 bug-fix wave and still redeemable in the current rotation.

steps.title: RELEASE - 10k Yen (Active)
steps.description: Launch-era LOCKED 2 code that remains useful for quick starter currency.

steps.title: Reward Type - Yen Currency
steps.description: Yen is used for LOCKED 2 customization decisions and stat-focused progression upgrades.

steps.title: Redeem Path - Menu > Store > Codes
steps.description: Finish the tutorial, press M or open the menu, then enter each LOCKED 2 code in the Store panel.

steps.title: Expired Pool - CODE, 70KLIKES, MEDALXLOCKED
steps.description: These names are already out of rotation and should be tracked only as historical LOCKED 2 references.

quickTips: LOCKED 2 code redemption is case-sensitive, so paste each code exactly as written.
Redeem newly released LOCKED 2 codes quickly because rotation windows can be short.
Use your first Yen batch on role-friendly upgrades instead of random early rerolls.
Re-check this LOCKED 2 section after each update wave for new additions.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: LOCKED 2 Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: New LOCKED 2 players improve fastest by layering fundamentals in order: tutorial completion, core inputs, stamina pacing, then role-specific flow and build choices.

items.name: Clear the LOCKED 2 tutorial first
items.description: Tutorial completion unlocks menu loops including Store access and code redemption, so it is your first progression gate.

items.name: Use PC for your first serious session
items.description: The early LOCKED 2 learning flow is usually smoother on PC, especially during initial tutorial objectives.

items.name: Drill four core actions early
items.description: Prioritize LMB dribble, RMB shot, Shift sprint, and Q slide tackle to build a stable first-match rhythm.

items.name: Treat stamina as a tactical resource
items.description: Use V to recover, then burst with Shift only when you create space, a tackle lane, or a shot window.

items.name: Layer Flow, Weapon, and Trait in sequence
items.description: After movement stabilizes, add G Flow, R Weapon, and T Trait timing so your actions stay deliberate under pressure.

items.name: Build around role identity and dribble style
items.description: LOCKED 2 rewards role clarity, so align your build with your lane responsibilities before chasing advanced combos.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: LOCKED 2 Controls
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: These LOCKED 2 keybind clusters keep field and goalkeeper actions in one place so you can practice inputs without losing match tempo.

solutions.name: Field Core Offense
solutions.description: Dribble with LMB, shoot with RMB, sprint with Shift, and use Q slide tackle for possession contests.

solutions.name: Field Utility and Ability Keys
solutions.role: V | G | R | T | M | CTRL
solutions.description: Manage stamina with V, trigger G Flow, R Weapon, T Trait, open menu with M, and toggle shift lock with CTRL.

solutions.name: Goalkeeper Save Basics
solutions.description: Use F standing block plus C and Z dives to protect both sides of goal during close-range pressure.

solutions.name: Goalkeeper Ball Control
solutions.description: Secure with E pickup, smother with Q forward dive and grab, then restart play through RMB punt timing.

managementTips: Drill field and goalkeeper controls in separate sessions to avoid input confusion in live matches.
Keep Shift lock ready for steadier camera control during pressure defense sequences.
Use stamina recovery proactively so sprint timing stays available for decisive actions.
Rebind only after baseline comfort; constant remapping slows LOCKED 2 muscle-memory growth.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: LOCKED 2 Best Weapons Right Now
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: The April 2026 rebuild turned LOCKED 2 weapons into active abilities, so timing matters as much as rarity. This tier read focuses on current impact, role fit, and how easily each weapon converts into match value.

cards.name: S Tier - Meta-Defining Weapons
cards.description: Primal Handstand, Serpent, and Emperor. These picks shape possessions and direct finishing outcomes in most ranked matches.

cards.name: A Tier - Strong and Stable
cards.description: Crushing Header, Riptide Curve, and Immense Speed. Flexible high-value weapons that hold up across multiple LOCKED 2 roles.

cards.name: B Tier - Good With the Right Setup
cards.description: Power Shot, Super Fast Scissors, Black Hole Trap, and Bear Claw. Good value when your timing, team shape, and role context are already clean.

cards.name: C Tier - Niche or Execution-Heavy
cards.description: Direct Shot, Bunny, Stealthy Steps, and Dragon Header. Playable options that need stricter timing or narrower match scripts.

cards.name: D Tier - Low-Impact Picks
cards.description: Stylish Reach, Monster Lift, and Big Bang Drive. They can work in labs, but deliver lower match impact than higher tiers.

highlights: Fast read: Primal Handstand leads defense, Serpent and Emperor lead direct finishing, and Riptide Curve stays the safest all-purpose option.
Default to S and A tier when climbing unless your LOCKED 2 team plan needs a niche interaction.
Use B and C tier only when your build already supports their timing windows and follow-up routes.
Re-check this LOCKED 2 weapon tier module after every balance update or cooldown adjustment.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: LOCKED 2 Best Traits to Keep
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: The best LOCKED 2 traits solve real role problems: movement, flow access, box finishing, steals, and goalkeeping control. Keep traits that give immediate match value instead of tiny stat bumps.

regions.name: Core Keep Traits
regions.description: Athlete, Puppeteer, Egoist, and Meta-Vision. Top-priority keeps with immediate impact across most LOCKED 2 compositions.

regions.name: Strong Keep Traits
regions.description: Golden Zone, Long Strides, Quick Draw, Calculate, and Destruction. Strong upgrades when they align with your weapon and role lane.

regions.name: Role Pick Traits
regions.description: Bunnys, Claw, Tank, Surf, Consistent, and Ripper. Strong in specific lanes such as goalkeeper, defender, or dribble-bait play.

regions.name: Farm or Specialist Traits
regions.description: No Look, Diver, Ambidextrous, Fortune, Tireless, and Powerhouse. Playable picks with narrower value than top general keeps.

regions.name: Reroll Traits
regions.description: Heavy, Dwarf, Weak, Lazy, and Tired. These LOCKED 2 traits reduce practical match value and should be replaced quickly.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: LOCKED 2 Copyable Role Builds
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: A strong LOCKED 2 build is not just a rare roll. Start from one clear role duty, then match the weapon plan, trait priorities, height bias, and flow timing into a repeatable win condition.

creatures.name: LOCKED 2 Beginner All-Rounder Build
creatures.description: Weapon priority: Riptide Curve then Immense Speed. Trait core: Athlete, Long Strides, Puppeteer. Keep angles simple, circulate cleanly, and attack half-spaces with control.

creatures.name: LOCKED 2 Striker Finisher Build
creatures.description: Weapon priority: Emperor then Serpent. Trait core: Quick Draw, Egoist, Golden Zone, Athlete. Save Flow for last-defender duels, rebounds, and box entries.

creatures.name: LOCKED 2 Winger Creator Build
creatures.description: Weapon priority: Riptide Curve, Super Fast Scissors, then Immense Speed. Trait core: Long Strides, Bunnys, Calculate, Puppeteer. Attack wide space for separation and delivery value.

creatures.name: LOCKED 2 Defense Anchor Build
creatures.description: Weapon priority: Primal Handstand, Bear Claw, then Stealthy Steps. Trait core: Meta-Vision, Tank, Claw, Surf. Hold Flow for emergency recovery, goal-line defense, and first-duel resets.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: LOCKED 2 Dribbling Without Turnovers
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Good LOCKED 2 dribbling is camera control, spacing, and stamina discipline first. Fancy clips come later; clean first touches and the correct angle win more possessions.

items.name: Turn On Shift Lock First
items.description: LOCKED 2 uses Left Ctrl for Shift Lock by default. Change it in Settings and Keybinds before you start serious dribbling practice.

items.name: Build Your Basic Ball Chain
items.description: Your base chain is simple: LMB keeps the dribble alive, Shift controls pace, RMB is your shot, and aerial RMB is your headbutt touch.

items.name: Dribble Into Space, Not Into The Defender
items.description: Take your next touch away from pressure instead of straight into a body. Cushion spacing forces the defender to turn before challenging.

items.name: Use Ground Control Before Air Tricks
items.description: Ground dribbles are the safest first beat. Go airborne only after a pop-up, rebound, or scramble where the extra touch changes the angle.

items.name: Protect Your Stamina Before Long Carries
items.description: Do not start a long dribble on a drained bar. Reset, recharge, then burst so you still have a final step or finish.

items.name: Use Flow After You Win The First Duel
items.description: Flow is strongest when it extends an advantage you already created. Beat the first marker first, then convert into a shot or final pass.

items.name: End Every Carry With A Purpose
items.description: Every carry should end with one of three outcomes: shot, pass, or safe reset touch. Recycle possession when none is open.

unlockMilestones: Toggle Shift Lock before your first duel and keep camera orientation stable.
Beat the first defender with spacing before adding extra dribble layers.
Finish carries with a shot, pass, or safe reset instead of forced duels.
Use LOCKED 2 Flow only after advantage creation, not from a standing touch.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: LOCKED 2 Weapons Guide
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: The April 2026 LOCKED 2 weapon update turned weapons into active abilities, so timing now matters as much as rarity.
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Use this guide when you want more than a raw tier list. Each card tells you what the weapon actually does, where it shines, and which role gets the most value from it.

items.name: Primal Handstand
items.summary: A massive-hitbox weapon that is currently one of the strongest defensive tools on the field.
items.playstyle: Box control, emergency saves, and last-man coverage

items.name: Serpent
items.summary: A charged long-range shot that can also be used mid-air, giving elite scoring pressure from multiple angles.

items.name: Emperor
items.summary: Grants instant shot charge and is one of the cleanest finishing weapons in the current meta.
items.playstyle: Fast release finishing inside or around the box

items.name: Crushing Header
items.summary: A high-impact aerial attack that makes crowded penalty-box finishes much easier.

items.name: Riptide Curve
items.summary: A very safe and versatile option for curved shots and support passing.

items.name: Immense Speed
items.summary: Builds speed over time until you are extremely fast, which is especially strong on shorter characters.

items.name: Power Shot
items.summary: A charged shot whose power scales well with your build, making it stronger in developed loadouts.

items.name: Super Fast Scissors
items.summary: A mobility-heavy weapon with built-in i-frames that helps extend combos and escape pressure.

items.name: Black Hole Trap
items.summary: A reworked timing-based weapon that stays effective if you master the activation window.
items.playstyle: Trap timing and controlled possession plays

items.name: Bear Claw
items.summary: Guarantees a catch on activation and has a forgiving low cooldown, making it one of the most reliable defensive tools.

items.name: Direct Shot
items.summary: A timing-based shot that becomes extremely fast when released at the perfect moment.
items.playstyle: Clean first-time finishing and timing skill checks

items.name: Stealthy Steps
items.summary: Gives a speed burst and partial invisibility, with more value in repositioning than pure attack.

items.name: Bunny
items.summary: Raises your jump enough to help shorter builds compete better in the air.

items.name: Dragon Header
items.summary: A fast, powerful header-style finish that is punishing if your timing is off.

items.name: Stylish Reach
items.summary: Temporarily enlarges your hitbox for interceptions, but it rarely swings a match on its own.

items.name: Monster Lift
items.summary: Lifts the ball while moving and works best when chained with other abilities instead of standing alone.
items.playstyle: Setups, feints, and follow-up ability chains

items.name: Big Bang Drive
items.summary: Offers aerial control and delayed-shot potential, but the slow animation makes it easy to read.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: LOCKED 2 Traits Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: LOCKED 2 traits add an extra build layer, from small stat shifts to rare match-changing passives.
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: This module is built for players who want to understand the trait pool instead of just memorizing names. Open a section to see the drop rate, the main effects inside that rarity, and the kind of playstyle that benefits most from those rolls.

items.title: Common Traits
items.summary: The most common roll pool, mostly made up of small stat changes and early reroll results.

content.label: Included traits
content.value: Dwarf, Lazy, Tired, Weak, None

content.label: What they change
content.value: Hitbox size, speed, stamina, shot power, or no bonus at all.

content.label: Quick read
content.value: Dwarf shrinks your hitbox; Lazy lowers speed; Tired lowers stamina; Weak lowers shot power.

items.title: Rare Traits
items.summary: The everyday utility tier for role tuning and reliable stat shaping.

content.label: Included traits
content.value: Long Strides, Heavy, Powerhouse, Tank, Surf, Clamps

content.label: What they change
content.value: Sprint speed, shot power, hitbox size, slide tackle distance, and M2 range.

content.label: Best use
content.value: Long Strides helps pace, Surf helps defensive reach, Clamps helps ball-winning, and Powerhouse or Heavy add more shot threat.

items.title: Exotic Traits
items.summary: A much stronger pool with cooldown help, specialist movement, and role-specific utility.

content.label: Included traits
content.value: Unbreakable, Fortune, Ripper, Bunnys, Quick Draw, Consistent, No Look, Athlete, Tireless, Claw, Diver

content.label: What they change
content.value: Ankle safety, extra cash in auto games, longer ragdolls, more jump, 50% faster shot charge, 15% cooldown reduction, boosted no-shiftlock shots, multi-stat boosts, more stamina, steal chance on blocks, and faster goalkeeper dives.

content.label: Best use
content.value: Quick Draw and Consistent fit active ability builds, Athlete is a broad all-rounder, Claw rewards defensive reads, and Diver is a pure goalkeeper trait.

items.title: Legendary Traits
items.summary: Low-drop passives that can reshape how your entire build plays around ego, shots, hitboxes, or team support.

content.label: Included traits
content.value: Golden Zone, Egoist, Meta-Vision, Ambidextrous, Puppeteer

content.label: What they change
content.value: Golden Zone buffs shots from its zone, Egoist gives 4x Ego, Meta-Vision increases hitboxes and protects against ankle breaks while active, Ambidextrous can empower shots from the glowing leg side, and Puppeteer charges Flow for five nearby teammates.

content.label: Best use
content.value: Golden Zone and Ambidextrous reward finishers, Meta-Vision helps ball control and coverage, and Puppeteer is the most team-oriented roll in the pool.

items.title: Exclusive Traits
items.summary: The rarest trait tier, built around signature abilities linked to specific weapon styles.

content.label: Included traits
content.value: Destruction, Dragon, Twin Guns, Calculate, Revolver

content.label: What they change
content.value: Destruction powers up the next Riptide, Dragon adds a strong header activation, Twin Guns creates a feint-and-real Direct Shot sequence, Calculate gives a speed buff through Riptide passing, and Revolver dashes you in the camera direction.

content.label: Best use
content.value: These rolls are strongest when your build already leans into Riptide, Direct Shot, heading, or trap-and-burst play patterns.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: LOCKED 2 Flow Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: Flow is a core LOCKED 2 power system that combines an active match state, a rolled stat bonus, and a visible variant.
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Open this module when you want to understand both sides of Flow: the actual stat roll and the visual identity attached to it. In current Locked 2, Yen can be spent on Flow-related unlocks, and the active Flow state is still one of the biggest swing moments in a match.

items.name: Flow State Basics
items.summary: Flow is a temporary active state used during matches.
items.details: Activate it with G. Every Flow roll gives a bonus between 5% and 15% in one category: Strength, Speed, Stamina, Cooldown, or Hitbox.
items.bestWith: Any role, because the value comes from the stat category you rolled.

items.name: Speed Flow State
items.summary: A speed roll increases how fast your player moves during the active Flow window.
items.details: The boost is rolled inside the 5% to 15% range.
items.bestWith: Wingers, recovery defenders, and any build that wins through separation.

items.name: Strength Flow State
items.summary: A strength roll raises the physical power of your Flow window.
items.details: The boost is rolled inside the 5% to 15% range.

items.name: Stamina Flow State
items.summary: A stamina roll helps you stay effective longer while Flow is active.
items.details: The boost is rolled inside the 5% to 15% range.
items.bestWith: Midfielders, wide runners, and high-movement roles.

items.name: Cooldown Flow State
items.summary: A cooldown roll improves how quickly your build cycles back into its tools.
items.details: The boost is rolled inside the 5% to 15% range.
items.bestWith: Active-ability players who want cleaner repeat windows.

items.name: Hitbox Flow State
items.summary: A hitbox roll gives you a larger active presence during the Flow window.
items.details: The boost is rolled inside the 5% to 15% range.

items.name: Puzzle
items.summary: Puzzle pieces hover around your player while Flow is active.
items.bestWith: Players who want a clean playmaker-style visual.

items.name: Lightning
items.summary: Green-and-black lightning cracks around the player during Flow.
items.bestWith: Aggressive, direct builds that want a high-energy visual.

items.name: Demon Wings
items.summary: Skeletal demon wings appear on your back with a red-pink aura.
items.bestWith: Aerial or chaos-finishing builds that like a loud visual profile.

items.name: Genius
items.summary: Numbers and white contrails surround your player while Flow is active.

items.name: Vines
items.summary: Vines and rose-petal-like trails wrap around the character during Flow.
items.bestWith: Creative attacking builds and stylish forwards.

items.name: Monster
items.summary: Glowing eyes and a fiery trail give your Flow a more feral look.

items.name: Snake
items.summary: Two different-colored auras split across the character model.
items.bestWith: Defensive-minded players who want a standout visual.

items.name: Wild Card
items.summary: Flames and four ace cards float around your player.
items.bestWith: Finisher builds that want a dramatic Flow look.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: LOCKED 2 Position Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: This LOCKED 2 position guide focuses on role responsibilities, useful weapon directions, and the inputs you rely on most.
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: A good position guide should tell players how to behave on the pitch, not just where to stand. Use these sections to match your role with the right responsibilities, button habits, and weapon direction.

items.title: Striker
items.summary: Your job is to finish moves quickly and turn good service into goals.

content.label: Main job
content.value: Attack the box, time the final run, and release shots before the defense resets.

content.label: Weapon directions
content.value: Emperor for fast finishing, Serpent for long-range pressure, Crushing Header for aerial service, and Direct Shot for timing-based releases.

content.label: Key inputs
content.value: RMB to shoot or charge, RMB in the air for a header touch, R for your weapon, and G to activate Flow.

content.label: What usually goes wrong
content.value: Taking one extra touch in traffic and letting a clean chance turn into a block.

items.title: Winger
items.summary: You create width, carry the ball into better angles, and force the back line to move.

content.label: Main job
content.value: Beat the first marker, stretch the pitch, and turn wide possessions into crosses, cut-ins, or recoveries.

content.label: Weapon directions
content.value: Immense Speed for long carries, Super Fast Scissors for movement and i-frames, Riptide Curve for delivery, and Bunny for extra aerial utility.

content.label: Key inputs
content.value: LMB to keep the dribble alive, Shift to run, RMB to shoot or cross from your angle, and R or G when you find the gap.

content.label: What usually goes wrong
content.value: Drifting inside too early and killing the width your team needs.

items.title: Midfielder
items.summary: A midfielder starts attacking sequences and helps balance attack with defense.

content.label: Main job
content.value: Initiate plays, connect phases, and keep the team stable between attack and defense.

content.label: Weapon directions
content.value: Riptide Curve for support passing, Power Shot for late-shot threat, Black Hole Trap for control, and Super Fast Scissors for escape routes.

content.label: Key inputs
content.value: LMB for carries, RMB for distribution or shots, Q for defensive challenge, and G or R when you need to swing momentum.

content.label: What usually goes wrong
content.value: Holding the ball too long and leaving the rest of the shape exposed.

items.title: Defender
items.summary: A defender stalls the play, buys time for teammates to recover, and steals when the moment is right.

content.label: Main job
content.value: Protect the dangerous lane, delay the attack, and win the ball without opening the whole back line.

content.label: Weapon directions
content.value: Primal Handstand for huge defensive reach, Bear Claw for clean catches, Stealthy Steps for recovery movement, and Stylish Reach for extra interception coverage.

content.label: Key inputs
content.value: Q to slide tackle, LMB and movement for body positioning, R to activate your weapon, and G when a defensive swing window appears.

content.label: What usually goes wrong
content.value: Diving too early instead of forcing the attacker into a worse angle.

items.title: Goalkeeper
items.summary: You are the last stop and the fastest way to reset your team after a save.

content.label: Main job
content.value: Read the shot, protect the near post, collect loose balls, and restart play cleanly.

content.label: Weapon directions
content.value: Primal Handstand is the standout choice for pure save coverage, while Bear Claw is a very reliable lower-cooldown option.

content.label: Key inputs
content.value: F for standing block, C and Z for dives, E to pick up the ball, Q for the forward dive and grab, RMB to punt, plus R and G for weapon and Flow.

content.label: What usually goes wrong
content.value: Committing to the dive before the shot path is fully shown.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: LOCKED 2 Height Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: LOCKED 2 height choices shape speed, stamina, shot power, and reach. Use this side-by-side split to pick a build that matches your role before you spend Yen on rerolls.

comparison.bestFor: Ball carrying, recovery runs, chasing opponents, and speed-led playstyles
Finishing, long shots, physical presence, and stronger box contests

notes: LOCKED 2 short builds are usually easier for high-tempo pressing and fast recovery lanes.
LOCKED 2 tall builds trade pace for stronger shot outcomes and wider contest coverage.
Use Menu > Teleport > Customisation > Physical > Roll Height when you need to switch profile focus.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: LOCKED 2 Goalkeeper Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: LOCKED 2 goalkeeping rewards fast reads and clean inputs. Drill the core save kit until each choice is automatic, then focus on secure possession and early distribution.

steps.title: Set LOCKED 2 camera control first
steps.description: LOCKED 2 uses Left Ctrl for Shift Lock by default. Remap it in Settings > Keybinds before queueing if needed.

steps.title: Memorize the six core goalkeeper actions
steps.description: Keep your save options simple: standing block for body-line shots, side dives for corners, front dive for loose touches, then pickup and punt to restart.

steps.title: Match each save to the shot line
steps.description: Use F when the finish stays central, use C or Z for wider lanes, and use Q when attackers over-dribble into your claim range.

steps.title: Secure possession and clear early
steps.description: After each clean stop, use E to lock the ball first and RMB to punt before pressure closes around your box.

steps.title: Use defensive weapons that hold up in LOCKED 2
steps.description: Primal Handstand and Bear Claw remain the most dependable goalkeeper picks for stable defensive coverage under pressure.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: LOCKED 2 Tournament Mode
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: LOCKED 2 tournament runs are built around queue entry, event coordination, and disciplined execution. Use this flow to arrive prepared for competitive rounds.

steps.title: Join through the LOCKED 2 tournament queue
steps.description: Use the central lobby queue point for tournament entry rather than standard casual lanes.

steps.title: Check official LOCKED 2 event updates before queuing
steps.description: Review the official Discord announcements so your team has the latest event window, rules, and bracket notes.

steps.title: Lock keybinds and camera settings in advance
steps.description: Confirm Shift Lock and role controls before entering to avoid setup mistakes during live rounds.

steps.title: Bring reliable active weapons

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: LOCKED 2 Update Log
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: This LOCKED 2 timeline highlights the major shifts that changed how players build teams, use weapons, and track live updates.

timeline.title: LOCKED 2 inherited the core football control foundation
timeline.summary: The original loop already combined player controls, goalkeeper controls, dribbling, shooting, flow, weapons, traits, stamina, and shift lock in live match play.
highlights: M1 dribble and M2 shoot control baseline
Flow, weapon, trait, and stamina systems existed before LOCKED 2
Tournament spaces were part of the earlier competitive structure

timeline.title: LOCKED 2 launched as a ground-up rework
timeline.summary: The launch build positioned LOCKED 2 as a rebuilt version focused on refined systems, improved performance, and a stronger competitive pace.
highlights: Ground-up rebuild
Refined systems
Stronger competitive focus

timeline.title: LOCKED 2 weapons shifted from passive to activatable
timeline.summary: Manual timing became essential as weapons moved away from passive boosts and toward activation-based impact.
highlights: Bear Claw guarantees a catch on activation
Power Shot became a charge-based finisher
Primal Handstand rose as a premium defensive option

timeline.title: Early LOCKED 2 weapon meta formed quickly
timeline.summary: Initial tier tracking settled around defensive stability and instant finishing, with several picks becoming early standards.
highlights: Top tier: Primal Handstand, Serpent, Emperor
High-value follow-ups: Crushing Header, Riptide Curve, Immense Speed
Defensive value stabilized faster than expected

timeline.title: Official Discord became the live LOCKED 2 update hub
timeline.summary: Live announcements, build discussion, and event updates moved fastest through the community Discord workflow.
highlights: Live announcements
Build discussion
Event updates

timeline.title: Launch-week LOCKED 2 codes delivered early Yen rewards
timeline.summary: Launch tracking confirmed a first reward wave that supported early customization and reroll planning.
highlights: RELEASE = 10k Yen
BUGFIX01 = 10k Yen
TY5KLIKES = 15k Yen

faq.title: LOCKED 2 Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before your next LOCKED 2 match session

questions.question: How do I redeem LOCKED 2 codes quickly?
questions.answer: Open Menu > Store > Codes after tutorial completion, then enter each active LOCKED 2 code exactly as listed.

questions.question: What should I learn first in LOCKED 2?
questions.answer: Start with core controls, stamina pacing, and safe role decisions before layering advanced dribbling and combo execution.

questions.question: Where can I find official LOCKED 2 updates?
questions.answer: Use the official LOCKED 2 Discord, Trello board, Roblox game page, and MOMENTXM group for first-party update channels.

questions.question: What is the safest LOCKED 2 build approach for beginners?
questions.answer: Use stable role-focused builds with reliable weapons and traits, then specialize once your fundamentals are consistent.

questions.question: How often should I revisit LOCKED 2 tier lists?
questions.answer: Re-check LOCKED 2 weapon and trait tiers after each update wave because balance shifts can quickly change practical value.

questions.question: How do I improve LOCKED 2 goalkeeper consistency?
questions.answer: Practice angle discipline, deliberate dive timing, and cleaner distribution choices after each save.
